title: Builders FirstSource (BLDR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Builders FirstSource (BLDR) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5% growth rate balances the structural long-term demand for US housing with the inherent cyclicality of the construction market and sensitivity to interest rates.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9% discount rate reflects the cyclical risk profile of the building materials sector, incorporating a standard equity risk premium over the current risk-free rate.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A conservative 2% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation expectations and historical baseline economic growth, acknowledging the mature nature of the domestic housing market.

thesisParagraphs: Builders FirstSource is uniquely positioned to capitalize on the chronic undersupply of housing in the United States. Through aggressive consolidation, the company has transformed into an indispensable partner for national and regional homebuilders, offering unmatched scale and a growing portfolio of value-added services that streamline construction.

While the stock remains sensitive to macroeconomic shifts and mortgage rate fluctuations, its underlying cash generation capability is robust. Management's proven ability to navigate cyclical downturns while continuing to execute accretive acquisitions and share repurchases suggests the market may be undervaluing its long-term earning power relative to pure-play commodity distributors.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 5% growth rate for Builders FirstSource?
faq.answer: The 5% rate is a normalized projection that accounts for both the strong secular tailwinds in US housing demand and the cyclical headwinds of interest rate volatility and commodity pricing pressure.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for BLDR's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate was applied, reflecting the cyclicality and economic sensitivity inherent in the building materials and residential construction sectors.

faq.question: Does this analysis account for lumber price volatility?
faq.answer: Lumber volatility is factored into the discount rate and the conservative growth projections. BLDR's strategic shift toward value-added products (like prefabricated components) is designed to mitigate some of this raw commodity exposure.
